fault_hypothesis: Lines 77-78 incorrectly pop the last element when both inputs share the same final edge, but the docstring says both copies must be retained. This post-processing removal is wrong and should be deleted.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true; when both sides share a final edge both copies must be kept.
After initialization merged__1 must be empty before any elements are appended.
The then-branch of the if at line 77 should never execute because popping a shared final edge is always wrong.
Before clamping, the merged list must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements.
